title: RNA Isolation from Human Intervertebral Disc Tissue
author: Nashrah Ahmad

steps_raw.instruction: Tissue collection and storage
Human lower thoracic and entire lumbar spines obtained from an organ procurement organization should be processed within 48 hours post-mortem.

After resecting IVDs from the spine, separate AF and NP, cut into smaller pieces and immediately place them in 4 ml of Allprotect® in 5 ml Eppendorf tubes and store at 4°C overnight, then place into -80°C for long-term storage.
Removal of Allprotect®, weighing, and tissue preparation
Thaw the tissue.
Gently remove residual Allprotect® using a Kimwipe and briefly rinse the tissue with PBS.
Cut off a section from the tissue of approximately target amount. For AF, use ~50 mg; for NP, ~200 mg and weigh. Cut additional slice as necessary.

Snap-freeze the tissue in liquid nitrogen and transfer it to a new tube on dry ice (or keep in LN₂) until further processing. NOTE: Scale up subsequent reagent volumes proportionally for NP. The same pulverization and homogenization procedure applies to both AF and NP. Lysate from NP tissue was divided across four columns after the ethanol precipitation step.
Mechanical pulverization
Pre-cool the tissue pulverizer (cell crusher) with LN₂.
Place the frozen tissue into the pre-cooled cell crusher, add LN₂, and pulverize to a fine powder using a hammer.
Rotate the cell crusher after each impact for thorough pulverization.
Continue until a fine powder is achieved. This procedure should be done quickly so that the cell crusher and tissue do not warm up.
Homogenization in TRIzol™
Transfer the pulverized tissue to a 2 mL tube containing 1 mL of ice-cold TRIzol™ using a clean spatula.
Vortex for 20 seconds, then place on ice for 1 minute. Repeat 2 more times.
Incubate the samples on a rotator at 4°C for 20 minutes.
Centrifuge at 12,000 rpm at 4°C for 30 seconds.
Transfer the supernatant (~1 mL) to a new pre-cooled 2 mL tube and store at −80°C or proceed to the next step.
First extraction
Add 200 μL of chloroform to the homogenate in TRIzol™.
Shake vigorously for 15 seconds and incubate for 2 minutes at room temperature (RT).
Centrifuge at 12,000 rpm at 4°C for 10 minutes.
Carefully collect the upper aqueous phase and transfer it to a new microcentrifuge tube.
Second extraction
Add 200 μL of chloroform to the aqueous phase.
Shake, incubate for 2 minutes at RT, and centrifuge at 12,000 rpm at 4°C for 10 minutes.
Collect the upper aqueous phase and transfer it to a new microcentrifuge tube.
Third extraction
Add 200 μL of chloroform to the aqueous phase.
Shake, incubate for 2 minutes at RT, and centrifuge at 12,000 rpm at 4°C for 10 minutes.
Collect the final upper aqueous phase and transfer it to a new microcentrifuge tube.
Ethanol precipitation
Add an equal volume (1:1) of 70% RNase-free ethanol (e.g., 300–400 μL) to the aqueous phase.
Mix gently and incubate on ice for 10 minutes.
Binding to the RNeasy column
Load 700 μL of the sample onto an RNeasy spin column.
Centrifuge for 15 seconds at >8,000 rpm at RT; discard the flow-through.
Repeat until the entire sample has passed through the column.
Note: For NP samples divide the sample into four parts and load onto four RNeasy spin columns to prevent clogging.
First wash
Add 350 μL of Buffer RW1 to the spin column.
Centrifuge for 15 seconds at >8,000 rpm at RT; discard the flow-through and the collection tube.
DNase treatment (first round)
Prepare the DNase reaction by adding 70 μL of DNA digestion buffer (Zymo) to 10 μL of Zymo DNase I (total 80 μL).
Pipette the entire 80 μL directly onto the column membrane.
Incubate for 15 minutes at RT.
Second wash
Add 350 μL of Buffer RW1 to the spin column.
Centrifuge for 15 seconds at >8,000 rpm at RT; discard the flow-through and the collection tube.
DNase treatment (second round)
Prepare another DNase reaction: add 70 μL of DNA digestion buffer (Zymo) to 10 μL of Zymo DNase I (80 μL total).
Pipette the 80 μL directly onto the column membrane.
Incubate for 15 minutes at RT.
Third wash
Add 350 μL of Buffer RW1 to the spin column.
Centrifuge for 15 seconds at >8,000 rpm at RT; discard the flow-through and the collection tube.
Buffer RPE washes
Transfer the spin column to a new collection tube.
Add 500 μL of Buffer RPE.
Centrifuge for 15 seconds at >8,000 rpm at RT; discard the flow-through.
Add another 500 μL of Buffer RPE.
Centrifuge for 2 minutes at >8,000 rpm at RT; discard the flow-through.
Final drying spin
Place the RNeasy column in a new 2 mL collection tube.
Centrifuge for 2 minutes at full speed (16,000 × g) at RT to dry the membrane thoroughly.
Elution and storage
Transfer the RNeasy column to a 1.5 mL microcentrifuge tube.
Add 15 μL of RNase-free water to the membrane and incubate for 1 minute.
Centrifuge at >8,000 rpm for 15 seconds at RT.
Return the same 15 μL eluate onto the column membrane, incubate for 1 minute, and centrifuge again for 1 minute at >8,000 rpm at RT.
Store the eluted RNA at −80°C until use.
Spectrophotometric analysis
Apply 1 μL of RNA to a NanoDrop™ 1000 (NanoDrop Technologies) to measure RNA concentration and purity.
Integrity analysis
Use 2 μL of RNA on the 4150 TapeStation System (Agilent Technologies) to determine RNA Integrity Number (RIN).
